facts: A penny is worth 1 cent and a quarter is worth 25 cents; because 25 is more than 1, a quarter has more value than a penny.
A dollar bill is paper money, not a coin; it has more value than a single quarter because you can exchange one dollar for four quarters, but one quarter cannot buy as much as a dollar.
Value tells how much something is worth; a coin with a larger number (like 25 for a quarter) has more value than a coin with a smaller number (like 1 for a penny).
You need 100 pennies to equal the value of one dollar bill, but one penny alone is not worth a dollar; this shows the dollar bill has more value than a single penny.
misconceptions: A penny is worth more than a dollar bill because the penny is made of metal and feels heavier, so it must be more valuable.
A quarter is worth the same as a penny because both are coins and you can use them the same way to buy things.
